message:
fix(core): gate lock-order tracking behind cfg(debug_assertions)

The lock-order enforcement module used variables (level, held) that
were only read inside #[cfg(debug_assertions)] blocks, but the
variables themselves were defined unconditionally. This caused
unused-variable warnings in release builds and wasted a thread-local
RefCell<Vec> allocation that served no purpose without the assertions.

Changes:
- Gate `use std::cell::RefCell` behind #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
- Prefix parameters with _ in check_before_acquire, did_acquire,
  did_release so they compile cleanly in both profiles
- Add #[cfg(debug_assertions)] to the out-of-order panic test since
  it relies on the debug-only tracking infrastructure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
